sourceMovie.title: Dragon Blade
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Dragon Blade (2015) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jack the Giant Slayer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Blade clicked for you, Jack the Giant Slayer (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Flyboys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Blade clicked for you, Flyboys (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and history, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragon Blade?
faq.answer: Try Jack the Giant Slayer, Flyboys, The Warrior's Way—each shares DNA with Dragon Blade while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragon Blade?
faq.answer: Dragon Blade (2015) is best described as Adventure, History, Action, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragon Blade worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
